questions.q: For the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math>, the other exremity of the focal chord which is drawn at the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
questions.solution:
a=2

⇒t1=±12

2 at1=2

by (1) & (2)  t1=1/2

for focal chord t1t2=-1

⇒t2=-2.

other point at22,2at2=(8,-8)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Through the vertex $\mathrm{O}$ of a parabola $\mathrm{y}^{2}=4 \mathrm{x}$, chords OP and $O Q$ are drawn at right angles to one another. The locus of the middle point of $\mathrm{PQ}$ is
questions.solution:
Given parabola is $\mathrm{y}^{2}=4 ...(1)
\mathrm{x}$ Let $\mathrm{P} \equiv\left(\mathrm{t}_{1}^{2}, 2 \mathrm{t}_{1}\right)$ and $\mathrm{Q} \equiv\left(\mathrm{t}_{2}^{2}, 2 \mathrm{t}_{2}\right)$
Slope of $\mathrm{OP}=\frac{2 \mathrm{t}_{1}}{\mathrm{t}_{1}^{2}}=\frac{2}{\mathrm{t}_{1}}$ and slope of $\mathrm{OQ}=\frac{2}{\mathrm{t}_{2}}$
Since $\mathrm{OP} \perp \mathrm{OQ}, \therefore \frac{4}{\mathrm{t}_{1} \mathrm{t}_{2}}=-1$ or $\mathrm{t}_{1} \mathrm{t}_{2}=-4 \ldots(2)$
Let $\mathrm{R}(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ be the middle point of $\mathrm{PQ},$ then $\mathrm{h}=\frac{\mathrm{t}_{1}^{2}+\mathrm{t}_{2}^{2}}{2} \quad \ldots(3) \quad$ and $\quad \mathrm{k}=\mathrm{t}_{1}+\mathrm{t}_{2} \ldots(4)$
From $(4), \mathrm{k}^{2}=\mathrm{t}_{1}^{2}+\mathrm{t}_{2}^{2}+2 \mathrm{t}_{1} \mathrm{t}_{2}=2 \mathrm{~h}-8 \quad[\mathrm{From}$
(2) and (3)] Hence locus of $\mathrm{R}(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ is $\mathrm{y}^{2}=2 \mathrm{x}-8$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: What is the area of the triangle formed by the lines joining the vertex of the parabola $x^{2}=12 y$ to the ends of the latus rectum?
questions.solution: Given parabola is $\mathrm{x}^{2}=12 y$ which is of the form $\mathrm{x}^{2}=4 a y$. $\Rightarrow 4 a=12 \Rightarrow a=3$ Now, $L M$ is the latus rectum whose length $=4 a=4 \times 3$ =12 So, area of $\Delta L M V=\frac{1}{2} \times L M \times V F$. $=\left(\frac{1}{2} \times 12 \times 3\right)$ sq. unit $=18$ square unit
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let $P$ be the mid-point of a chord joining the vertex of the parabola $y^{2}=8 x$ to another point on it. Then, the locus of $P$ is
questions.solution: Let the chord be MN. Let other end of chord joining from vertex to it lying on $y^{2}=8 x$ be $N\left(2 t^{2}, 4 t\right)$ $\therefore$ Mid-point of $M N=\left(t^{2}, 2 t\right)$ $\therefore \quad x=t^{2}, y=2 t \Rightarrow x=\left(\frac{y}{2}\right)^{2}$ $\Rightarrow \quad y^{2}=4 x$ is the required locus of $P$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For the parabola $\mathrm{y}^{2}=4 \mathrm{x}$, the point $P$ whose focal distance is 17 , is
questions.solution:
Given, $\mathrm{y}^{2}=4 \mathrm{x}$
Let $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ be any point on the parabola
$\therefore \quad(\mathrm{h}-1)^{2}+(\mathrm{k}-0)^{2}=17^{2}$
Also, $\mathrm{k}^{2}=4 \mathrm{~h}$
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{h}^{2}+1-2 \mathrm{~h}+4 \mathrm{~h}=289$
$\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{h}^{2}+2 \mathrm{~h}-288=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad(\mathrm{h}+18)(\mathrm{h}-16)=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{h}=16 \quad(\because h$ cannot be negative $)$
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{k}^{2}=64$
$\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{k}=\pm 8$
$\therefore$ Points are $(16,8)$ or $(16,-8)$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The focal distance of a point on the parabola $y^{2}=16 x$ whose ordinate is twice the abscissa, is
questions.solution:
Given curve is $y^{2}=16 x$. Let the point be $(h, k)$. But $2 h=k$, then $k^{2}=16 h$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow & & 4 h^{2} &=16 h \\
\Rightarrow & & h &=0, h=4 \\
\Rightarrow & & k &=0, k=8
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Points are $(0,0),(4,8)$.
Hence, focal distance are respectively
$$
0+4=4,4+4=8
$$
$(\because$ focal distance $=h-a)$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of the curve whose vertex is $(0,0)$ and length of latusrectum is $\frac{16}{3}$, is
questions.solution: For the curve $3 y^{2}=16 x$, the length of latusrectum is $\frac{16}{3}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The sum of the reciprocals of focal distances of a focal chord $P Q$ of $y^{2}=4 a x$ is
questions.solution:
Equation of parabola $y^{2}=4 a x$
Total length of focal chord $=4 a$
and half length of focal chord $=2 a$
Now, sum of the reciprocals of focal distances
$$
=\frac{1}{2 a}+\frac{1}{2 a}=\frac{2}{2 a}=\frac{1}{a}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The focus of the curve $y^{2}+4 x-6 y+13=0$ is
questions.solution:
The given equation of curve is:
$$
y^{2}+4 x-6 y+13=0
$$
which can be written as: $\mathrm{y}^{2}-6 \mathrm{y}+9+4 \mathrm{x}$
$$
\begin{array}{l}
+4=0 \\
\Rightarrow\left(y^{2}-6 y+9\right)=-4(x+1) \\
\Rightarrow(y-3)^{2}=-4(x+1)
\end{array}
$$
Put $Y=y-3$ and $X=x+1$
On comparing $Y^{2}=4 a X$
Length of focus from vertex, $a=-1$
At focus $X=a$ and $Y=0 \Rightarrow x+1=-1$
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=-2 \\
\therefore \mathrm{y}-3=0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{y}=3 \\
\therefore \quad \text { Focus is }(-2,3)
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The length of the chord of the parabola $y^{2}=4 a x(a>0)$ which passes through the vertex and makes an acute angle $\alpha$ with the axis of the parabola is
questions.solution: Hint: Equation of OP:- $y=x \tan \alpha$ Solving with $y^{2}=4 a x$, we get : $x^{2} \tan ^{2} \alpha=4 a x \quad \Rightarrow x=4 a \cot ^{2} \alpha$ Substituting, $y=4 a \cot \alpha$ $\therefore \mathrm{P} \equiv\left(4 \mathrm{a} \cot ^{2} \alpha, 4 \mathrm{a} \cot \alpha\right)$ So, $\overline{\mathrm{OP}}=\sqrt{16 \mathrm{a}^{2} \cot ^{4} \alpha+16 \mathrm{a}^{2} \cot ^{2} \alpha}=4 \mathrm{a} \cot \alpha \operatorname{cosec} \alpha\left(\right.$ as $0^{\circ} < \alpha < 90^{\circ}$, so $\left.\cot \alpha>0, \operatorname{cosec} \alpha>0\right)$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math> <mrow><mo>(</mo> <mrow> <mn>9</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>a</mi> </mrow> <mo>)</mo></mrow> </math> is a point bounded in the region formed by parabola <math> <mrow> <msup> <mi>y</mi> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mo>=</mo><mn>16</mn><mi>x</mi> </mrow> </math> and <math> <mrow> <mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>,</mo> </mrow> </math> then
questions.solution:
Since, the point ( 9a,6a ) is bounded in the region formed by the parabola y 2 =16 and x=9, then



y2−16x<0, x−9<0

⇒36a2−169a<0, 9a−9<0

36a(a−4)<0,  9a−9<0

0<a<4, a<1⇒0<a<1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: In the adjacent figure, a parabola is drawn to pass through the vertices B,C and D of the square ABCD. If A(2,1), C(2,3), then the focus of this parabola is:
questions.solution:
We observe that  x-coordinate of  A and C is same.

Hence, the diagonal AC is parallel to y-axis and its mid-point is (2,2).

Now, other diagonal will be parallel to x-axis as given quadrilateral is square.

Thus, B≡(3,2) and D≡(1,2).

Now, equation of parabola can be assumed as :

x-22=λy-3

Since it passes through B3, 2, so

1=-λ⇒λ=-1

∴x-22=-y-3

Hence, axis is x-2=0.

y-coordinate of focus is y-3=-14

∴ Focus ≡2, 114.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The length of the latus rectum of a parabola whose focal chord $P S Q$ is such that $P S=3$ and $Q S=2$ is
questions.solution:
We know that the length of the semi latus rectum is the harmonic mean of focal radii, so length of the latus rectum
$$
=2\left(\frac{2(P S)(Q S)}{P S+Q S}\right)=2 \times \frac{2 \times 3 \times 2}{3+2}=\frac{24}{5}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $b$ and $c$ are the lengths of the segments of any focal chord of a parabola $y^2=4 a x$, then the length of the semi-latus rectum is :
questions.solution:
Since the semi latus rectum of a parabola is the harmonic mean between the segments of any focal chord of the parabola. $\therefore l$ is the harmonic mean between $b$ and $c$. Hence,
$l=\frac{2 b c}{b+c}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let the focus <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> of the parabola <math><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=8</mo><mi>x</mi></math> lies on the focal chord <math><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> of the same parabola. If <math><mi>P</mi><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the square of the slope of the chord <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
We know that, PS, 4, SQ are in H.P.
⇒16,14,1SQ are in A.P.
⇒1SQ=24-16=13⇒SQ=3
⇒PQ=6+3=9=4cosec2θ (where, tan⁡θ is the slope of PQ )
⇒1+cot2⁡θ=94⇒cot2⁡θ=54
⇒tan2⁡θ=45
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi></math> for which the curve <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>λ</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>24</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> represents a parabola, is
questions.solution:
Given, curve is

7x+52+7y+32=λ24x+3y-242,

⇒7x+572+7y+372=λ242+322×4x+3y-2442+322,

⇒49 x+572+y+372=25λ24x+3y-2442+322,

⇒x+572+y+372=25λ2494x+3y-2442+322,

⇒x+572+y+372=5λ7×4x+3y-2442+32.

The L.H.S. of the above equation represent distance of a point x,y from a point -57,-37 and R.H.S. represent its perpendicular distance from a line 4x+3y-24=0.

Hence, the above equation satisfy the basic definition of a conic section, i.e., PS=ePM, where PS is the distance of a variable point from a fixed point (focus) and PM is its perpendicular distance from a fixed line (directrix) and e is the eccentricity.

And, we know that, for parabola e=1.

∴λ=75, ⇒λ=±75.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of the parabola with the focus $(3,0)$ and the directrix $x+3=0$, is.
questions.solution:
Given that focus is $S(3,0)$, let $P(x, y)$ be any point on the parabola.
$\therefore$ Directrix is, $x+3=0$
Also, $S P^2=P M^2$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad(x-3)^2+(y-0)^2=\left(\frac{x+3}{\sqrt{1}}\right)^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad(x-3)^2+y^2=(x+3)^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad y^2=(x+3)^2-(x-3)^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad=(x+3+x-3)(x+3-x+3) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad y^2=2 x 6=12 x
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the line <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math> bisects two chords of the parabola <math><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi></math> which are passing through the point <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then the length of the latus rectum can be equal to
questions.solution:
Any point on y=x–1 can be taken as h,h–1
Taking h,h-1 as the midpoint of chord, the equation of chord is yh-1-4bx+h2=h-12-4bh
Which passes through the point b,-2b
⇒ -2bh-1-2bb+h=h2-2h+1-4bh
⇒ h2-2h+2b2-2b+1=0
As, above equation has two real roots, hence, 
-22-412b2-2b+1>0
⇒ 2b2-2b<0⇒b<1⇒4b<4
⇒ length of latus rectum is less than 4
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math> be a point on the parabola, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>12</mn><mi>x</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> be the foot of the perpendicular drawn from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> , on the axis of the parabola. A line is now drawn through the mid-point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>N</mi></math>, parallel to its axis which meets the parabola at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math>. If the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo></math>intercept of the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NQ</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then :
questions.solution:
Comparing the equation of the parabola, with standard equation y2=4ax, we get a=3.

Figure is drawn on the basis of the given information.

Parametric form of parabola is x=at2, y=2at

∴ P=3t2,6t, N=3t2,0, M=3t2,3t

⇒Q=3t24,3t

Equation of NQ:  y-0=3t-03t24-3t2x-3t2

i.e., y=-43t·x-3t2⇒y=-43t·x+4t

⇒4t=43⇒t=13

PN=6t=2

MQ=3t2-3t24=9t24=14
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The locus of the point <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> whose distance from the line <math><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></math> is equal to the distance from <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, is a parabola with the length of latus rectum same as that of the parabola <math><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Length of LR=2SM
=2 22+2-022+1=125
⇒1K=125⇒K=512
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
